transcript.clean_text: the riders of the tour de france reconvened after the second rest day to begin stage 16 high up in andorra crossing the border in cold conditions from pastela casa the border town with they'd france heading to san gordon for the 15th time in the history of the tour de france 169 kilometers first of three consecutive days in the pyrenees in the end of the tour four categorized climbs on the way an intermediate sprint as well and over three thousand meters of climbing on the downhill start to the stage it would be kespa askren who'd be trying to get up the road michael kowski would join him with mattia cathagno on the first climb of the day but even after 65 kilometers the break was not done they were chased back at the end of descent number one a couple of splits in the bunch but all the important riders making them then another group would go 14 riders would end up getting up the road intermediate sprint taken by three of them with matthews and colbrelli sprinting on for points behind both of those riders getting closer to mark cavendish who was being protected safely by his teammates in the groupeto behind patrick comrade would be trying to make things difficult in the breakaway christian yenten up the road as well 14 riders never really getting too established together always a split or two here and there on the final climb of the day the finest serious climb that is the call deported it would be comrade who'd accelerate the austrian champion only ever having won is national championships two wins the latest coming just a month ago one by one they tried to get organized behind and chase gordu and colbrelli would do that but they wouldn't see comrade again a nervy moment on a descent with some very tragic historical past for both the leader and colbrelli chasing on almost a minute of a gap though with 15 kilometers to go comrade looking good but if they got organized behind that could have been an opportunity to chase him down that wouldn't be the case the last fourth category climb of the day came in seven kilometers from the finish and conrad would ride away despite accelerations behind there'd be a real chance to enjoy the victory as well a big margin going into the final kilometer over a minute and patrick conrad making a real name for himself after years having played domestique he enjoyed it his team enjoyed it a second stage win for borah hansgroh following on from neil's polits win just a week before second place went to colbrelli but another chance to win a stage went missing third to michael matthews perez the frenchman in fourth and fifth top ten all made up of the breakaway riders in the meantime behind in the peloton it looked as though it was a pretty calm day until what fanart had looked to try and put a little bit of pressure on with his team at jonas finger it seemed like a strange move at the time all the riders in the top 10 present and all coming across the line together today pogaccha keeping the yellow jersey leading uran by 518 vinegar and carapace unchanged in third and fourth o'connor in fifth with kellerman just behind top 10 riders all staying the same unlikely to be the case however going into the next stage leaving murray and heading up to salah sula heading above salary for a second ever finish at call duponte scene of a big victory for naido quintana a couple of years previous 178 kilometers to ride then the first of two mountain top finishes back to back in the pyrenees the tour de france in play right here right now you
